description: Based on record 1.2.1: User interrupts assistant mid-confirmation at turn 2 (user audio_start while assistant audio still open). After interruption, user provides full flight change request, triggering a failed tool call. Assistant asks for last name, then turn 3 succeeds with correct tool calls. Tests interruption detection, skip_next_turn_increment keeping continuation in same turn, [assistant starts replying - user interrupts] prefix, and turn alignment with tool calls.

description: Based on record 1.2.3: Assistant interrupts user at turn 3. User starts spelling confirmation code (audio_start arrives slightly before pipecat turn_start, in the previous turn's window). Assistant audio_start arrives while user audio is still open — a real interruption where the assistant cuts in mid-speech. Tests that user_audio_started_in_current_turn persists from the previous turn window (no false negative), skip_next_turn_increment keeps continuation in same turn, and [assistant interrupted user] prefix is applied.

description: Based on record 1.1.5/trial_0 turns 8-9. ElevenLabs delivers user_speech for the next turn BEFORE audio_start(elevenlabs_user), but AFTER the assistant has responded to the previous turn. Tests that user_speech is buffered and replayed at the correct turn (9) instead of being appended to the previous turn (8). The key difference from a late transcript is that the assistant responded in between, so this is a new speaking session.
